title: Service Outcomes, Boundaries, and Server Lifecycle · Go Semantics Case: Startup Readiness Dependency
summary: A civic incident API has endpoints but no stated user outcome, ownership boundary, startup contract, dependency readiness, shutdown policy, or evidence connecting a request to durable effect. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo startup readiness dependency increment in service outcome, boundary, and lifecycle map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate configuration, bind the intended listener, initialize dependencies, expose liveness separately from readiness, and fail startup atomically.
Distinguish process, listener, connection, stream, request, handler, goroutine, dependency, and durable-state lifetimes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Startup Readiness Dependency · Theory Startup Readiness Dependency
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1543 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate listener liveness, dependency readiness, and draining state; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
